game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Red's First Flight Red's First Flight is a physics-based puzzle game where players launch Red, or other objects, using a slingshot mechanic to hit targets or solve environmental puzzles within each level. Gameplay relies on precise trajectory and destruction physics, with levels having specific objectives (e.g., hitting all pigs, collecting stars) and often limited attempts or resources.
game_info.description: Launch Red the bird using a slingshot to destroy structures and hit all the green pigs to complete each level. Pull back the slingshot to aim and release to launch. Each level has limited birds to use - plan your shots carefully! Destroy structures strategically to make them collapse onto pigs for maximum effect.
game_info.controls:
SPACE: Pull back slingshot / Release to launch
Arrow Keys (LEFT/RIGHT): Adjust aim angle while pulling back
Arrow Keys (UP/DOWN): Adjust launch power while pulling back
ENTER: Start game from start screen
ESC: Pause game
R: Restart (returns to start screen on game over)
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests basic game functionality with random sticky key inputs to ensure the game runs without errors and responds to player controls properly.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Randomly cycles through game control keys (SPACE, arrow keys) with each key held for several frames before switching, simulating continuous player input.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Game runs without crashes or errors, slingshot responds to inputs, birds launch when SPACE is released, and game continues through multiple attempts without breaking.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>

<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests that the player can successfully win the game by hitting all pigs in a level using optimal shot placement and timing.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Aims carefully at structural weak points to cause maximum collapse damage, targeting pigs directly or structures above them to make debris fall on pigs.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>All pigs are eliminated within the available bird count, level completes successfully, and game transitions to WIN state.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>

<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests losing condition by intentionally missing all shots to verify game properly handles failure state.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Launches all available birds with poor aim (too high or to the side) to miss all targets and structures completely.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>After using all birds without hitting all pigs, game transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE state with appropriate message.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>

<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests precise aiming mechanics by attempting to hit specific weak points in structures with controlled power and angle adjustments.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Uses arrow keys to fine-tune angle and power, targeting specific structural joints or supporting blocks to cause maximum damage with minimal birds.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Precise control over trajectory is demonstrated, structures collapse as intended when hit at weak points, showing physics system working correctly.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>

<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests rapid input changes to ensure the slingshot aiming system handles quick adjustments without errors or visual glitches.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Rapidly alternates between different arrow keys while pulling back the slingshot, then releases to verify aim calculations are accurate despite quick input changes.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Slingshot visual feedback remains smooth, final trajectory matches displayed aim line, and birds launch correctly despite rapid input changes during aiming.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
